---
title: "Investing in Intelligence: A Real Options Framework for AI Compute Infrastructure"
date: today
date-format: long
format:
  html:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.html
  pdf:
    output-file: ai_lab_investment.pdf
authors:
  - name: Vincent Grégoire
    affil-id: 1
    affiliation:
      - ref: 1
affiliations:
  - id: 1
    name: HEC Montréal
abstract: |
  I develop a unified model of irreversible capacity investment under demand uncertainty with regime switching, oligopoly competition, endogenous default risk, and diminishing returns calibrated to AI scaling laws.
  The model delivers: (i) semi-analytical characterization of optimal investment triggers and capacity in a duopoly with default risk, where firms choose when and how much to invest facing a Tullock contest for AI compute market share; (ii) numerical solutions for $N$-firm sequential equilibrium with dynamic training-inference allocation; and (iii) a "revealed beliefs" methodology that inverts the model to extract AI labs' private beliefs about transformative AI arrival from observable capital expenditure.
  Calibrated to stylized versions of Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and CoreWeave, the model reveals substantial dispersion in implied arrival-rate beliefs.
  I introduce the "Dario dilemma"---the asymmetric cost of belief mismatches---showing that aggressive overinvestment carries higher downside risk than conservative underinvestment, rationalizing extreme capital commitments only under genuinely optimistic beliefs.
keywords:
  - real options
  - AI infrastructure
  - regime switching
  - strategic investment
  - default risk
  - revealed beliefs
thanks: "I thank Charles Martineau for helpful comments. Vincent Grégoire: HEC Montréal (vincent.gregoire@hec.ca)."
---


{{< include _introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include _model.qmd >}}

{{< include _extensions.qmd >}}

{{< include _calibration.qmd >}}

{{< include _valuation.qmd >}}

{{< include _discussion.qmd >}}

{{< include _conclusion.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

{{< include _appendix.qmd >}}

\newpage
\clearpage

## References {.unnumbered}

\begingroup
\raggedright
::: {#refs}
:::
\endgroup
